title: Counting & Cardinality
objective: Students will explore counting & cardinality concepts including Skip Counting (4s, 8s, 50s, 100s), Counting Within 1,000.

steps.content: Let's explore Skip Counting (4s, 8s, 50s, 100s)! Have you ever wondered about this?
Count from 0 in multiples of 4, 8, 50, and 100
Let's practice together! Recite the multiples of 4 from 0 to at least 48
Can you count in fours all the way to 48, count in eights, or count up in hundreds from any starting point — for example 350, 450, 550?
Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Skip Counting (4s, 8s, 50s, 100s). Recite the multiples of 4 from 0 to at least 48 Recite the multiples of 8 from 0 to at least 96
Today we're going to learn about Counting Within 1,000. Get ready for an adventure!
Count within 1000, including skip-counting by 5s, 10s, and 100s
Let me show you something cool. Count forwards and backwards within 1000 from any starting number
Can you count confidently up to 1000 — and if you ask them to count by hundreds from 200, or by tens from 650, can they do it without losing their place?
You did it! Here's what we explored about Counting Within 1,000: Count forwards and backwards within 1000 from any starting number Skip-count by 5s from any multiple of 5 to 1000
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Picture & Bar Graphs!

Draw a picture graph and a bar graph (with single-unit scale) to represent a data set with up to four categories; solve put-together, take-apart, and compare problems using information presented in a bar graph
Let me show you something cool. Draw a picture graph with a symbol representing one unit for each data point
Can you draw a simple bar chart or picture graph to show information — like how many of each colour of sweet are in a bag — and answer questions from it?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Picture & Bar Graphs: Draw a picture graph with a symbol representing one unit for each data point Draw a bar graph with labelled axes and a single-unit scale
